questions.question: How much moderate exercise per week is recommended for anxiety reduction?

options.text: 30 minutes total per week
150 minutes per week (about 30 minutes, 5 days)
4 hours per day
Exercise doesn't affect anxiety

questions.explanation: Research shows that 150 minutes of moderate exercise per week (about 30 minutes, 5 days per week) is associated with nearly 25% reduced risk of depression and anxiety. Exercise effects can rival psychotherapy and medication for mild-to-moderate anxiety.

questions.question: Why should you avoid caffeine in the afternoon if you have anxiety?

options.text: Caffeine is only effective in the morning
Caffeine can disrupt sleep, and poor sleep increases anxiety
Afternoon caffeine tastes different
It's more expensive in the afternoon

questions.explanation: Caffeine should be avoided at least 6 hours before bed (generally after 2pm) because it can disrupt sleep. Since poor sleep increases anxiety, and anxiety disrupts sleep, caffeine can worsen this cycle. Caffeine also directly increases anxiety by blocking the relaxation neurotransmitter adenosine.

questions.question: What type of diet is associated with reduced anxiety according to research?

options.text: High-sugar, processed food diet
Mediterranean-style diet (fruits, vegetables, fish, olive oil, whole grains)
Liquid-only diet
Diet has no effect on mental health

questions.explanation: Research shows that Mediterranean diet adherence is inversely correlated with anxiety and depression. This diet emphasizes fruits, vegetables, whole grains, fish (especially fatty fish for omega-3s), olive oil, nuts, and legumes while limiting processed foods and refined sugars.

questions.question: Why does using alcohol to 'take the edge off' backfire for anxiety?

options.text: Alcohol has no effect on anxiety
It provides temporary relief but causes rebound anxiety ('hangxiety'), disrupts sleep, and increases dependence risk
It only backfires if you drink too much
Alcohol is always helpful for anxiety

questions.explanation: While alcohol may temporarily reduce anxiety, it creates rebound anxiety as it wears off ('hangxiety'), disrupts sleep quality, changes neurotransmitter levels, and significantly increases the risk of alcohol dependence—people with anxiety disorders have a 5x higher risk of developing dependence.

questions.question: What is the 'Worry Dump' technique for improving sleep?

options.text: Throwing away all your worries in the trash
Before bed, spend 10 minutes writing down every worry to get them out of your head and onto paper
Telling someone else all your problems
Avoiding any thoughts before bed

questions.explanation: The Worry Dump technique involves spending 10 minutes before bed writing down every worry on your mind—big or small. Don't try to solve them; just capture them on paper. This signals to your brain that the worries have been recorded and can be addressed tomorrow, allowing you to let go and sleep.

title: Daily Habits That Reduce Anxiety - Quiz
